content.text: A chat interface is the primary contract between an agentic system and its users. Unlike a simple chatbot, an agent chat UI must surface tool invocations, multi-step reasoning, citation links, streaming text, and error states — all without overwhelming the reader. This section covers the full anatomy of a production-grade agent chat interface: message rendering, tool-call visualization, session management, and the FastAPI + React patterns that wire everything together.

attrs.alt: Chat interface anatomy: session sidebar, user and AI message bubbles, tool-call pill, streaming indicator, and input box

content.text: Every message in the conversation has a role, content, optional tool calls, and metadata. Define these types on both the server and client so they stay in sync.

from __future__ import annotations
from typing import Literal, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import time, uuid

@dataclass
class ToolCallRecord:
    id: str
    name: str
    input: dict[str, Any]
    result: Any = None
    error: str | None = None
    started_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    ended_at: float | None = None

    @property
    def duration_ms(self) -> float | None:
        return (self.ended_at - self.started_at) * 1000 if self.ended_at else None

@dataclass
class ChatMessage:
    id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    role: Literal["user", "assistant", "tool"] = "user"
    content: str = ""
    tool_calls: list[ToolCallRecord] = field(default_factory=list)
    model: str = ""
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    input_tokens: int = 0
    output_tokens: int = 0

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "role": self.role,
            "content": self.content,
            "tool_calls": [tc.__dict__ for tc in self.tool_calls],
            "model": self.model,
            "created_at": self.created_at,
            "input_tokens": self.input_tokens,
            "output_tokens": self.output_tokens,
        }

The server exposes two routes: a POST endpoint that accepts a user message and returns a stream, and a GET endpoint that returns conversation history. SSE (Server-Sent Events) is the simplest transport for streaming responses.

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
import asyncio, json

app = FastAPI()
client = AsyncAnthropic()

SYSTEM = """You are a helpful research assistant. When you use tools,
briefly narrate what you're doing before and after each call.""\"

# In-memory store for demo; replace with Redis/Postgres in production
conversations: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}

@app.post("/chat/{session_id}/message")
async def send_message(session_id: str, body: dict, request: Request):
    history = conversations.setdefault(session_id, [])
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": body["content"]})

    async def event_stream():
        buffer = ""
        tool_calls: list[dict] = []
        tool_buffers: dict[int, dict] = {}

        async with client.messages.stream(
            model="claude-opus-4-8",
            max_tokens=4096,
            system=SYSTEM,
            messages=history,
            tools=TOOLS,
        ) as stream:
            async for event in stream:
                etype = event.type

                if etype == "content_block_start":
                    if event.content_block.type == "tool_use":
                        idx = event.index
                        tool_buffers[idx] = {
                            "id": event.content_block.id,
                            "name": event.content_block.name,
                            "input_raw": "",
                        }
                        yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type':'tool_start','name':event.content_block.name,'id':event.content_block.id})}\n\n"

                elif etype == "content_block_delta":
                    d = event.delta
                    if d.type == "text_delta":
                        buffer += d.text
                        yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type':'text','text':d.text})}\n\n"
                    elif d.type == "input_json_delta" and event.index in tool_buffers:
                        tool_buffers[event.index]["input_raw"] += d.partial_json

                elif etype == "content_block_stop" and event.index in tool_buffers:
                    tb = tool_buffers.pop(event.index)
                    tc_input = json.loads(tb["input_raw"] or "{}")
                    # Execute tool
                    result = await run_tool(tb["name"], tc_input)
                    tool_calls.append({"id": tb["id"], "name": tb["name"],
                                       "input": tc_input, "result": result})
                    yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type':'tool_result','id':tb['id'],'name':tb['name'],'result':str(result)[:400]})}\n\n"

                elif etype == "message_stop":
                    msg = await stream.get_final_message()
                    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": msg.content})
                    yield f"data: {json.dumps({'type':'done','input_tokens':msg.usage.input_tokens,'output_tokens':msg.usage.output_tokens})}\n\n"

    return StreamingResponse(event_stream(), media_type="text/event-stream",
                             headers={"X-Accel-Buffering": "no", "Cache-Control": "no-cache"})

@app.get("/chat/{session_id}/history")
async def get_history(session_id: str):
    return {"messages": conversations.get(session_id, [])}

The React component subscribes to the SSE stream, appends text deltas in real time, and renders tool-call cards inline within the assistant message. Keep the component state flat so re-renders stay cheap.

Tool calls should be visually distinct from prose. A collapsed pill shows the tool name and status; clicking it expands to show the full input and result. This keeps the chat readable while preserving full transparency.
A sidebar listing past conversations requires a session API. Each session stores its message history and a generated title (derived from the first user message).

import re
from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic

client = AsyncAnthropic()

async def generate_session_title(first_user_msg: str) -> str:
    """Generate a short title for a conversation from the first message."""
    result = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=20,
        system="Reply with a 3-5 word title for a conversation that starts with this message. No punctuation.",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": first_user_msg}],
    )
    return result.content[0].text.strip()

# FastAPI routes for session CRUD
@app.post("/sessions")
async def create_session():
    sid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    conversations[sid] = []
    session_titles[sid] = "New conversation"
    return {"id": sid, "title": "New conversation"}

@app.get("/sessions")
async def list_sessions():
    return [{"id": sid, "title": session_titles.get(sid, "Untitled")}
            for sid in conversations]

@app.delete("/sessions/{session_id}")
async def delete_session(session_id: str):
    conversations.pop(session_id, None)
    session_titles.pop(session_id, None)
    return {"ok": True}

# Auto-title on first message
async def maybe_set_title(session_id: str, user_msg: str):
    if session_titles.get(session_id) in (None, "New conversation"):
        title = await generate_session_title(user_msg)
        session_titles[session_id] = title
        return title
    return None
Users need feedback during latency. Show a typing indicator when the assistant has started responding but has not yet emitted any text. Also handle the cold start state (no messages yet).
Agent loops can fail mid-stream due to rate limits, context-length overruns, or tool errors. Surface these in-line with a dismissable error card rather than silently truncating the response.
Never re-render full message; append only
Show name + status; expand for input/output
Render only cited indices, not all results
Bouncing dots while awaiting first token
Inline error card with retry button
Auto-generate title from first user message
Only auto-scroll if user is near bottom
Forcibly scrolling to the bottom during streaming is annoying if the user has scrolled up to review earlier content. Detect whether the user is near the bottom before scrolling.
Unit-test the SSE parsing logic with synthetic event chunks; integration-test the FastAPI endpoint with httpx and a mock Anthropic client.

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_chat_streams_text():
    # Fake SSE data the mock client will yield
    fake_events = [
        'data: {"type":"text","text":"Hello"}\n\n',
        'data: {"type":"text","text":", world"}\n\n',
        'data: {"type":"done","input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":2}\n\n',
    ]

    async def fake_stream(*args, **kwargs):
        for chunk in fake_events:
            yield chunk.encode()

    with patch("app.event_stream", return_value=fake_stream()):
        async with AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test") as ac:
            async with ac.stream("POST", "/chat/sess1/message",
                                 json={"content": "Hi"}) as resp:
                chunks = []
                async for chunk in resp.aiter_text():
                    chunks.append(chunk)
    assert any("Hello" in c for c in chunks)

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_tool_call_flow():
    """Verify tool_start and tool_result events appear in the stream."""
    # Generate a response that triggers a tool call
    async with AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test") as ac:
        events = []
        async with ac.stream("POST", "/chat/sess2/message",
                              json={"content": "search for Python papers"}) as resp:
            async for line in resp.aiter_lines():
                if line.startswith("data: "):
                    events.append(json.loads(line[6:]))
    types = {e["type"] for e in events}
    assert "tool_start" in types or "text" in types  # depends on model response
: track cumulative token usage per session; warn the user when approaching the model's limit (~180k tokens for Claude) and offer to summarise or start a new session.
: cap the user's textarea at 32k characters; refuse silently larger pastes to avoid prompt-injection via tool results.
: SSE connections drop on mobile. Store partial messages in sessionStorage; on reconnect replay from last received event ID using the Last-Event-ID header.
: add aria-live='polite' on the message container so screen readers announce new assistant text without interrupting every token.
: never load all messages on mount. Paginate from the bottom (newest first); prepend older messages as the user scrolls up.
: debounce the send button; disable it while a stream is in-flight to prevent duplicate submissions.

A production agent chat UI combines SSE streaming, inline tool-call pills, session management, and careful error handling. The key patterns are: stream text deltas directly into the DOM without full re-renders; show tool calls as expandable pills embedded in the assistant turn; auto-generate session titles; guard auto-scroll to respect user navigation; and surface errors inline with retry affordances rather than silent failures.
